{"query":"Super Bowl LX final score winner MVP Kenneth Walker III Seattle Seahawks New England Patriots February 8 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1713,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Walker was named Super Bowl LX MVP in the Seahawks' 29-13 win over the Patriots on Sunday at Levi's Stadium, rushing for 135 yards on 27 carries and catching two passes for 26 yards to total 161 scrimmage yards.Feb 8, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.nfl.com/news/seahawks-rb-kenneth-walker-iii-named-super-bowl-lx-mvp#:~:text=Walker%20was%20named%20Super%20Bowl,to%20total%20161%20scrimmage%20yards.","title":"Seahawks RB Kenneth Walker III named Super Bowl LX MVP","content":"Walker was named Super Bowl LX MVP in the Seahawks' 29-13 win over the Patriots on Sunday at Levi's Stadium, rushing for 135 yards on 27 carries and catching two passes for 26 yards to total 161 scrimmage yards.
He's the first running back to win the award since Terrell Davis for the Broncos in the Super Bowl XXXII upset over the Packers on Jan.
The fourth-year back really stepped up for the Seahawks in the playoffs, rushing for four touchdowns in the two games prior to the Super Bowl and taking on a heavier workload after running mate Zach Charbonnet tore his ACL in the Divisional Round win over the 49ers and missed the remainder of the postseason."},{"url":"https://www.seahawks.com/news/super-bowl-title-mvp-award-still-surreal-for-kenneth-walker-iii-one-day-later","title":"Super Bowl Title & MVP Award ‘Still Surreal’ For Kenneth Walker III One Day Later","content":"Feb 9, 2026 — Walker was named MVP of Seattle's 29-13 win in Super Bowl LX after he rushed for 135 yards on 27 carries, adding two catches for 26 yards,
Seattle Seahawks running back Kenneth Walker III holds the Pete Rozelle MVP trophy while posing during a news conference the morning after Super Bowl 60 between the Seahawks and the New England Patriots in San Francisco, Monday, Feb.
Walker was named MVP of Seattle's 29-13 win in Super Bowl LX after he rushed for 135 yards on 27 carries, adding two catches for 26 yards, making him the offensive standout of an otherwise defensive game.
Walker's 2025 season saw him eclipse 1,000 rushing yards for the second time in four years in the NFL, but he went through some ups and downs, as did all of Seattle's running game, before catching fire late in the year, eclipsing 100 scrimmage yards in five of Seattle's last six games, including 313 rushing yards, four rushing touchdowns and 104 receiving yards in three postseason games.
And as if winning the Super Bowl and earning MVP honors wasn't enough to make Sunday special for Walker, it also happened to be his father's first NFL game.

Walker was a force throughout the game, finishing the contest with 131 rushing yards and 26 receiving yards.
He waltzed in for the score.

Seahawks offense: 183 yards on 4.7 yards per play"},{"url":"https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/47872913/seahawks-rb-kenneth-walker-iii-named-mvp-super-bowl-lx","title":"Seahawks RB Kenneth Walker III named MVP of Super Bowl LX - ESPN","content":"Nick WagonerFeb 8, 2026, 10:42 PM ET
On Sunday night, Walker played so well he was named the MVP of Super Bowl LX, leading the Seattle Seahawks to a 29-13 victory against the New England Patriots at Levi's Stadium.
Walker rushed for 135 yards on 27 carries against the Patriots, adding 26 receiving yards on two catches to become the first running back to win Super Bowl MVP since Denver's Terrell Davis in Super Bowl XXXII following the 1997 season.
When Charbonnet tore his left ACL in the divisional round against the San Francisco 49ers, all eyes turned to Walker.
After the game, Charbonnet wasn't surprised that Walker had earned MVP despite entering the game with the longest odds (+850) for a player to win it since Patriots receiver Julian Edelman in Super Bowl LIII."},{"url":"https://seahawkswire.usatoday.com/story/sports/nfl/seahawks/2026/02/08/seahawks-vs-patriots-kenneth-walker-iii-named-mvp-of-super-bowl-lx/88584744007/","title":"Seahawks vs Patriots: Kenneth Walker III named MVP of Super Bowl LX","content":"Kenneth Walker III was a force of nature for Seattle, carrying the ball 27 times for 135 yards."}]}
